item.title: CV
item.description: You can download a PDF copy of my CV here.

item.title: About
item.description: Thanks for stopping by. I’m a musicologist and (mostly amateur) musician currently living in the Boston area. My research focuses on music cultures in German-speaking lands and the United States during the long(est) 19th century. I’m especially interested in ways that cultural hierarchies interact in, and across, various repertoires. Among other topics, I have researched and written on: late 18th-century popular theater and its influence on chamber and concert music during the 19th century mutual influences between spoken theater and music how music streaming service users form virtual communities (link) how individuals form and express artistic identities, focused on Sophie Schröder structures of commendation and honor for 19th-century German musicians, focused on Clara Schumann gender representation in US-ian classical music radio programming (an ongoing collaborative project with my wife, Kathryn, which will soon be publicly hosted on this website) Links to representative work in these areas will be posted on this site soon.
